calendar.matters:
REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (815 Central Avenue, Pittsburg, Ca 94565)
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Bernard appeared via zoom for SFPG(petitioner).
Melanie Emmons Court appointed counsel appeared via zoom.
Traci Lewis appeared via zoom for sellers.

The sale was confirmed at $338,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed April 01, 2026. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property. Order sunder submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bloom. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (815 Central Avenue, Pittsburg, Ca 94565)
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Continued To May-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Katelyn Phillips appeared via zoom for San Francisco Public Guardian (petitioner).
Melanie Emmons appeared in person for conservatee.

Discussion was held regarding the issues with the sale, that the proof of publication indicates that the real property is located at 850 Central Ave, not 815 Central Ave, and that property has been re appraised for $372,000.00 so it passes the 90% value.

The matter is continued to May 26, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Petition To Waive Requirements Of San Francisco Local Rule 14.47.B.(5) Regarding Sale Of Trust Real Property For Good Cause, Or, In The Alternative, For Authority To Sell Trust Real Property And For Authority To Grant Exclusive Right To List And Sell Real Property Interest
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Katelyn Phillips for petitioner SF Public Guardian (via Zoom).
Melanie Emmons, court-appointed counsel for conservatee Margaret L. Cortez (via Zoom).

Ms. Phillips confirmed that there were no objections to the proposed sale. Ms. Emmons stated that her client also had no objections and supported the petition.

Ms. Phillips requested, in the alternative, that the Court waive the requirement of sale under court supervision in order to expedite closing, explaining that the beneficiary owed a significant balance to her care facility and that eviction proceedings had been discussed.

The Court indicated it was willing to authorize the sale but preferred that the sale proceed subject to court confirmation, allowing for potential overbids at confirmation.

The Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant authority to sell the trust property, enter into an exclusive listing agreement, and ordered that the sale be subject to court confirmation.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
